source.summary: Merged backlog from structured CI-adoption feedback and a later repo-fit assessment across utility files, store code, portability cases, and planted bugs through 0.1.14.
source.headline: CJ is strong on utility modules and crash detection, but needs better operator ergonomics and broader callable-surface visibility for modern TypeScript app code.

prioritization.strategy: The current backlog is cleared. The next planning pass should be driven by fresh user feedback, benchmark evidence, or product goals rather than the prior CI-adoption and callable-surface gaps.

phases.name: CI Trust Foundation
phases.goal: Make current outputs trustworthy enough to gate on in CI.

phases.name: Adoption Blockers
phases.goal: Give teams the controls and docs they need to keep CJ on in real workflows.

phases.name: Signal Refinement
phases.goal: Improve finding quality and machine-readable categorization without broadening scope too aggressively.

phases.name: Coverage And Workflow Expansion
phases.goal: Expand reach and package the experience once the core CI contract is stable.

phases.name: Operator Ergonomics
phases.goal: Reduce misleading output and make local triage usable without custom jq or Python.

phases.name: Declarative Semantics
phases.goal: Let users teach CJ about expected behavior instead of relying only on name-based heuristics.

phases.name: Callable Surface Expansion
phases.goal: Make CJ see meaningful logic in modern TypeScript code that lives behind classes, object literals, factories, and framework containers.

phases.name: Workflow Packaging
phases.goal: Package the stabilized gates and broader callable surface into a first-party CI workflow command.

tasks.title: Fix diff_scoped reporting when --diff-file is active
tasks.problem: Coverage JSON can report diff_scoped as null even when diff filtering clearly ran.
evidence: scraper-jobs.ts run reported diff_scoped: null while counts.skipped_diff_filtered was 9
deliverables: Reproduce the mismatch through the CLI JSON output path
Set diff_scoped deterministically whenever a diff was supplied
Add a regression test that covers --diff-file end to end
acceptance_criteria: coverage.detail.diff_scoped is true whenever --diff-file is provided and diff filtering is applied
CLI JSON output matches the internal verify-stage accounting
A targeted regression test fails before the fix and passes after it

tasks.title: Add schema_version to JSON reports and publish a stability contract
tasks.problem: CI and dashboard consumers have no machine-readable versioning signal for report shape changes.
evidence: User feedback requested a top-level schema_version field to prevent silent breakage on upgrades
deliverables: Add top-level schema_version to every JSON report
Document stage-name and JSON-key stability guarantees
Define how schema changes will be versioned across releases
acceptance_criteria: All report-producing commands emit schema_version at the top level
Docs state whether stage names are stable, append-only, or version-gated
Future schema-breaking changes have a documented bump process

tasks.title: Add report-level controls to cap artifact size
tasks.problem: Single-file JSON outputs are already large enough to create CI artifact bloat.
evidence: sn-normalize.json was 72KB
hotel-cache.json was 38KB for a single-file run
deliverables: Add --report-level minimal|full
Define which fields are retained in minimal mode
Document intended use in CI and local debugging workflows
acceptance_criteria: Minimal mode preserves pass/fail, findings, stage outcomes, and schema_version
Full mode remains backward-compatible with the current detailed output
Docs explain when to use minimal versus full reports

tasks.title: Split execute-stage severities so no_inputs_reached is diagnostic, not failing
tasks.problem: The execute stage can go ok=false even when the only issue is that generated inputs were all rejected.
evidence: buildSignedScrapeUrl rejected all 45 generated inputs and the execute stage still failed despite no crash
deliverables: Introduce a distinct no_inputs_reached outcome
Separate diagnostic severities from stage-failing severities
Update summary accounting and docs to reflect the new taxonomy
acceptance_criteria: All-inputs-rejected runs are visible in the report but do not fail the execute stage by default
Real crash findings still fail the execute stage
Regression coverage exists for the all-inputs-rejected case

tasks.title: Add a suppression or baseline mechanism for known findings
tasks.problem: Teams need a way to keep CI on without disabling entire stages after the first known false positive.
evidence: User feedback explicitly called out suppression as the main blocker to CI adoption
deliverables: Choose a suppression model: ignore file, inline directives, baseline snapshot, or a combination
Allow suppressions by path, stage, and function or finding key
Record suppressed findings explicitly in JSON output
acceptance_criteria: A known false positive can be suppressed without disabling execute globally
Suppressed findings remain auditable in the report
Docs explain precedence, matching, and update workflow

tasks.title: Add granular execute-stage gating controls
tasks.problem: Users can currently gate on the whole execute stage but not on finding severity classes.
evidence: Requested behavior: fail on crash severity only, allow property_violation, ignore no_inputs_reached
deliverables: Add an execute-gate flag or config surface
Define the supported execute severities and default gate behavior
Document common CI profiles
acceptance_criteria: Users can configure execute gating at severity granularity
The default gate remains sensible for local runs
The selected gating policy is visible in the report or CLI output

tasks.title: Publish a real CHANGELOG and release semantics notes
tasks.problem: Teams cannot easily tell when findings changed because tool semantics changed versus because code changed.
evidence: User feedback called out the silent determinism heuristic change and lack of visible version history
deliverables: Add CHANGELOG.md covering at least 0.1.7 through current
Call out semantic changes that can affect findings
Link release notes from the README or docs index
acceptance_criteria: Every release notes semantic shifts that can change findings or stage outcomes
A new user can find release history from the repo root
The changelog distinguishes bug fixes, output-shape changes, and benchmark/docs updates

tasks.title: Improve fused-flag parse errors for shell-script invocation mistakes
tasks.problem: When a flag and value are accidentally fused into one token, the CLI error is correct but not helpful.
evidence: Example: unknown flag '--diff-file /tmp/x.diff' when args were passed as a single token
deliverables: Detect common fused-flag forms in parse errors
Suggest the intended split tokens in the error message
Add regression tests for representative bad invocations
acceptance_criteria: The CLI suggests splitting the flag and value when it sees a fused flag token
Existing valid flag parsing behavior is unchanged
Tests cover at least --diff-file and one other path-valued flag

tasks.title: Structure portability output into machine-readable fields
tasks.problem: Portability failures are still mostly raw stderr, which is hard to aggregate and automate against.
evidence: Requested structure: failing_imports, reason, and fix_hint instead of a raw Node stack trace blob
deliverables: Parse known portability failure classes into structured fields
Preserve raw stderr for debugging without making it the primary interface
Document the portability taxonomy
acceptance_criteria: Common Node ESM/module-load portability failures are emitted as structured JSON
Raw stderr is still available in full reports
At least one fix_hint path is tested for known import-resolution failures

tasks.title: Expose cognitive complexity as a first-class metric and profile option
tasks.problem: Cyclomatic complexity alone underweights deeply nested control flow that humans experience as hard to maintain.
evidence: checkRelation measured cyclomatic 15 but cognitive 26 in user feedback
deliverables: Add a complexity-metric selector or equivalent config surface
Support cognitive complexity in thresholding and reporting
Decide whether the security profile should remain cyclomatic-based, become cognitive-based, or support both
acceptance_criteria: Users can choose cyclomatic or cognitive complexity for gating
Reports clearly state which metric drove a violation
Docs explain tradeoffs and default behavior

tasks.title: Classify wide-type parameter crashes separately from likely runtime bugs
tasks.problem: Some fuzz crashes only happen on values allowed by the type signature but never passed by any observed static call site.
evidence: hotel-cache.ts jsonResponse crashed on status: 0 even though real call sites only pass literals in a safe range
deliverables: Prototype a finding class like type_signature_wider_than_usage
Use observed static call-site values as an advisory signal, not as proof of safety
Generate remediation suggestions that prefer narrowing the type when appropriate
acceptance_criteria: The tool can distinguish at least one wide-type-only crash case from a general crash finding
The finding text makes clear that the classification is inferred from observed usage
A regression test covers the hotel-cache-style pattern

tasks.title: Auto-seed fuzz inputs from existing tests and literal call sites
tasks.problem: Generic generation misses valid boundary-shaping inputs that already exist in repo tests.
evidence: Requested examples included scrape-url.ts, where generators could not reach valid base URLs without help
deliverables: Search conventional nearby test paths automatically when not explicitly provided
Extract literal argument seeds from simple expect(fn(...)) or call-site patterns
Blend discovered seeds into fuzz generation without hiding purely generated cases
acceptance_criteria: CJ can discover and use argument seeds from at least one conventional test file layout
Reports indicate when seeded inputs contributed to coverage
Auto-seeding can be disabled for deterministic debugging

tasks.title: Add a no_fuzzable_surface classification for vacuous zero-arg coverage
tasks.problem: Some zero-arg, impure, env-gated functions currently count as fuzzed even when the run provided little or no real signal.
evidence: ensure-scraper.ts had two zero-arg exports that both passed 30x while coverage still reported fuzzed: 2
deliverables: Define a no_fuzzable_surface or equivalent coverage outcome
Detect vacuous zero-arg cases that only touch globals, env, or clocks without meaningful parameter exploration
Reflect the new classification in coverage summaries
acceptance_criteria: Vacuous zero-arg cases no longer overstate fuzz coverage
Legitimate zero-arg deterministic functions can still be marked as covered when appropriate
A regression test covers the ensure-scraper-style pattern

tasks.title: Ship a court-jester ci subcommand for first-party PR workflows
tasks.problem: Every adopter otherwise has to reinvent diff extraction, fan-out, aggregation, baseline comparison, and reporter formatting.
evidence: Requested UX: court-jester ci --base=origin/main --gate=complexity,portability --report=github
deliverables: Design a ci subcommand that scopes to changed files
Aggregate per-file JSON into a single gate result
Support at least one reporter format for PR annotations
acceptance_criteria: A user can point CJ at a base revision and get a single CI-oriented result
The command supports minimal output suitable for GitHub or similar CI surfaces
The implementation reuses stable report schema and gating primitives instead of inventing a second reporting path

tasks.title: Publish cost-curve guidance for PR-sized runs
tasks.problem: Potential adopters need an honest expectation for latency and artifact cost on real PRs.
evidence: Observed local run: 10 files in parallel in about 30 seconds, with an expected 30-file PR around 90 seconds
deliverables: Measure median and p95 run times for representative TypeScript and Python files
Document which stages dominate runtime
Add guidance for parallelism and report-level choice in CI
acceptance_criteria: Docs include at least median and p95 timing guidance
Users can estimate the cost of a 10-file and 30-file PR from the published guidance
The note calls out when execute-stage work dominates total runtime

tasks.title: Promote real repo wins and auditable-green examples in release and site copy
tasks.problem: The repo has strong concrete proof points, but they are not packaged into a durable user-facing adoption narrative.
evidence: CRLF-injected X-Correlation-Id crash in correlation.ts
nil-deref in authz/check.ts:buildCacheKey found through factory-to-nested fuzzing
3+ Node-strict ESM portability issues
Zero crashes across ~575 fuzz executions in security-boundary code with auditable coverage
deliverables: Add a short wins section to release notes, README, or site copy
Pair concrete bug finds with auditable-green examples
Make the examples easy to cite in future release notes
acceptance_criteria: At least one user-facing doc includes both bug-finding wins and auditable-green examples
The examples are specific enough to be credible without exposing sensitive repo details
Future releases have a place to carry forward similar proof points

tasks.title: Separate lint runner failures from actual lint issue counts
tasks.problem: Biome or Ruff runner failures can corrupt the top-line lint issue count and make the summary misleading.
evidence: User report: Biome returned internalError/io on a known existing file
The same run counted the failure as a lint_issue in the summary, corrupting the headline number
deliverables: Define a distinct runner-failure path for lint infrastructure errors
Keep summary.lint_issues scoped to actual diagnostics only
Add regression coverage for a Biome or Ruff execution failure that still leaves issue counts at zero
acceptance_criteria: Lint infrastructure failures appear as stage errors, not ordinary lint issue counts
summary.lint_issues reflects only diagnostics emitted by the linter
A regression test covers the failure-accounting split

tasks.title: Add a human-readable summary view for interactive triage
tasks.problem: The JSON report is good for CI plumbing but too cumbersome for interactive use without extra jq or Python.
evidence: User report: the report is comprehensive but not scannable for questions like top-5 complexity offenders
deliverables: Add either verify --summary human or a summarize subcommand for an existing report
Surface stage outcomes, top complexity offenders, top execute findings, and skipped-function counts
Document how the human summary relates to the stable JSON report contract
acceptance_criteria: A user can answer what failed and what to inspect first without custom scripting
The summary output is deterministic enough for local use but does not replace the JSON contract
Tests cover at least one mixed-failure report rendered through the summary path

tasks.title: Support source-level complexity suppressions
tasks.problem: Users can suppress complexity findings in a JSON file, but not at the source location that actually carries the noise.
evidence: User report: room.ts::webSocketMessage is noisy complexity signal and there is no way to add a suppression comment at source
deliverables: Choose a source-level directive syntax for complexity ignores
Associate directives with the following function or callable declaration
Keep suppressed findings visible in report detail and summary counts
acceptance_criteria: A complexity finding can be suppressed in source without muting the whole file or stage
Suppressed complexity findings remain auditable in the report
Regression tests cover both direct and adjacent comment-placement cases

tasks.title: Add declarative user-supplied property checks v1
tasks.problem: Property inference is currently hardcoded and name-driven, so silent wrong answers are invisible unless a built-in heuristic happens to match.
evidence: User report: absBroken was caught by a name-triggered non-negative check, while clampBroken, sortBroken, idempotentBroken, and reverseBroken sailed through
deliverables: Define a controlled vocabulary for declarative properties such as idempotent, sorted, permutation, nonnegative, clamped, nonempty_string, symmetric, and comparator
Support specifying those properties either inline or via a sidecar configuration path
Integrate declarative properties into the fuzz harness and report shape without breaking existing heuristic checks
acceptance_criteria: A user can mark a function with at least one declarative property without renaming the function
The generated harness enforces the requested property and reports failures through the existing execute-stage taxonomy
Docs explain the supported property vocabulary and precedence versus built-in heuristics

tasks.title: Expand callable discovery to exported object-literal methods and class methods
tasks.problem: CJ currently focuses on top-level named functions, missing a large amount of real logic in modern TypeScript modules.
evidence: User report: gameStore.ts is 343 lines of zustand store logic but CJ analyzed only 3 functions
The synthesizer currently filters to !is_method && !is_nested top-level candidates
deliverables: Extend analysis to emit stable qualified names for exported object-literal methods and class methods
Teach the synthesizer to build harness calls for those methods without treating every nested helper as public API
Update coverage reporting so the newly visible callables are counted explicitly
acceptance_criteria: At least exported object-literal methods and class methods can appear as first-class callable surfaces in parse and coverage output
The execute harness can invoke those callables with stable names and source locations
Regression tests cover at least one object-literal export and one class-method example

tasks.title: Promote factory-returned object methods into explicit callable coverage
tasks.problem: CJ already exercises some factory-returned methods indirectly, but they are not modeled as named callable surfaces with their own coverage and reporting identity.
evidence: Factory-to-nested fuzzing found buildCacheKey via createAuthzChecker, but the path is still implicit rather than a first-class callable surface
deliverables: Represent factory-returned callables with stable synthetic identities in coverage and execute output
Reuse the existing factory exercise machinery where possible instead of inventing a second harness model
Make it obvious when a finding came from a returned method versus the factory constructor itself
acceptance_criteria: Returned object methods show up as explicit callable coverage entries rather than only an aggregate factory note
Findings can identify the returned method that crashed or violated a property
Regression tests cover a factory that returns multiple callable methods

tasks.title: Handle framework and container closures such as zustand stores and hook bodies
tasks.problem: A large share of modern app logic lives inside closures passed to framework or container APIs rather than at top level.
evidence: User report: zustand create(set => ({...})) logic was effectively invisible
The same blind spot is expected for hook bodies, RxJS pipelines, and other factory-wrapped logic
deliverables: Design a container-aware callable-discovery model for common wrapper patterns instead of treating all nested closures as internal helpers
Start with at least one concrete container family such as zustand-style stores
Define how those discovered callables will be named, fuzzed, and reported without exploding into every incidental nested closure
acceptance_criteria: At least one real container pattern yields meaningful callable coverage beyond top-level functions
The report distinguishes intentional surfaced callables from incidental nested helpers
The implementation is framed as an explicit supported pattern, not a brittle generic AST hack
